text: it really has been a rough couple of
years for the MCU with an unpleasant
combination of a multiverse story arc
that never really got off the ground
disgraced stars that forced major course
changes box office flops disappointing
underperformers and occasional isolated
successes that were impossible to
capitalize on it's a franchise that's
been drifting directionless ever since
Endgame with no clear narrative drive
and nothing in particular to get excited
about and from the looks of things so
far 2025 isn't shaping up much better
what they really need is a good old
Avengers movie something to reunite the
scattered characters and wandering
storylines of the MCU into one big
blowout finale so that they can move
forward into a bold new era of
storytelling and the thing in question
is Avengers Doomsday scheduled to
release in May of next year well
actually it's the first part of that
thing the second part is going to be
Avengers Secret Wars in 2027 which if
the rumors are true will basically act
as the endgame to Doomsday's Infinity
War capping off the current era of
Marvel movies and probably feeding into
a soft reboot of the entire franchise
and not before time either now because
it's been quite a while since anyone got
excited for anything MCU related it was
vital that Marvel did everything they
could to build up the anticipation for
this one so what did they do you might
ask did they release some kick-ass
teaser trailer to get the audience
pumped up did they give tantalizing
hints about the exciting new direction
they'll take with the story maybe even
some interviews with the cast and
director
wrong nah they just dropped a 5-hour
live stream with a bunch of names on the
backs of
chairs i mean [ __ ] me it's different I
guess and to be fair there's a pretty
extensive list of names attached to this
one sure a lot of them are [ __ ]
nobodyies from obscure movies that most
of us can barely even remember seriously
you need to stop trying to make Yolena
happen she's not going to happen neither
is Shuri as Discount Black Panther for
that
matter however there are some big names
in there too once again proving that
every man has his price robert Downey Jr
is coming back after being away for more
than half a decade this time playing
Victor Bond Doom which I'm sure totally
isn't a cheap way of backdooring Tony
Stark into the franchise again if I was
a cynical man I'd say that old Robert
here might have been missing those big
Marvel paychecks lately because his
postendame career hasn't exactly been
jam-packed with box office smashes the
thing is as awesome as he was back in
the day he's almost 60 years old now and
he kind of looks it and I'm not really
sure I want to see a stringy washed out
aging veganized actor like him
half-heartedly going through the motions
for an easy payday it's a bit like
dragging your dad along to a Comic-Con
dressed as some anime character he's
never heard of and forcing him to pose
for pictures with people a third of his
age i'm actually kind of surprised to
see Chris Hemsworth coming back though
when he seemed to be pretty much done
with Marvel after the disaster of Love
and Thunder i wonder what they've got in
mind for him this time can't wait to see
Thor go on another journey of
self-discovery to find his true purpose
in life for like the fifth time in the
past decades pedro Pascal is also making
an appearance in this movie naturally
because he's in literally everything in
fact I'm not entirely convinced he isn't
in this room with me right now oh wait
that's just
Tatiana shut up i'll feed you when I'm
goddamn good and ready
no sign of Brie Larson as Captain Marvel
though is that like a personal attack or
something i mean I know she's been
overshadowed by people like Rachel Zgler
in recent years who did what she did
only bigger and faster and more
[ __ ] but you know what bri did it
before it was cool she walked so that
Rachel could fly and I think she
deserves closure damn it but the biggest
surprise if you can even call it that
was the contingent of old X-Men actors
making an appearance in this film and
[ __ ] me I really do mean old patrick
Stewart and Ian Mckllen who are both
well into their 80s now are reprising
their roles as Xavier and Magneto so are
Alan [ __ ] Rebecca Remain and James
Marsden i wonder if Rebecca is going to
have the balls to get into that blue
body suit again and you can bet your
left arch cheek that Hugh Jackman won't
pass up an opportunity to play Wolverine
again even if he hasn't been officially
announced yet the thing is haven't we
done the whole bring back the X-Men
actors for nostalgia bait routine like
several times already i mean I like
those actors as much as the next guy and
it feels like there's a growing
nostalgia for that early 2000s era of
superhero movies but really what are we
trying to accomplish here just another
multiversal cluster [ __ ] where we bash a
bunch of toys from different franchises
together and hope something interesting
happens and I guess that leads me into
my main takeaway from this whole thing
it all just feels a little bit desperate
just one big gimmick baiting people into
watching it for a chance to see their
favorite character in action one more
time and the thing is that's really not
what the Avengers movies are supposed to
be [ __ ] films like this are supposed to
be the culmination of a story line
that's been carefully building up for
several years across multiple movies
it's the big release of dramatic tension
that you need every so often but the
problem is there is no dramatic tension
in the MCU anymore because nobody seems
entirely sure what the [ __ ] even
happening in it captain America: Brave
New World mentioned at one point that
the Avengers technically don't even
exist anymore and it took me a moment to
realize oh yeah he's actually right
there it's been so long since any movie
in the franchise actually addressed the
state of the Marvel universe that I'd
stopped even thinking about it in those
terms anymore much like Star Wars can't
seem to make any new ideas work Marvel
is now forced to reference itself to
generate interest in its new projects
drawing on the past to try to prop up
the present like an old man with his
best days long behind him all the MCU
can really do now is reminisce about
happier times when it was still young
and cool and respected when it was still
doing great things and people flock to
see its latest offerings on their own
merits as I said earlier the consensus
seems to be that a reboot of some kind
is on the way for Marvel and the two
upcoming Avengers movies are going to
act as kind of closure for the
characters and stories that have been
driving it since the very beginning it's
the kind of thing the comics industry
does from time to time when storylines
have become so convoluted and
unmanageable that the only thing left to
do is wipe the slate clean and start
again and after almost two decades of
movies and TV shows hundreds of
characters and crazy universe changing
events the MCU really is sinking under
its own weight now that is an issue but
the sad reality is that its own audience
has kind of outgrown it and moved on now
the kids who excitedly flocked to the
cinema to watch that first Avengers
movie back in 2012 are now adults in
their 20s probably have kids of their
own by now and don't really have the
time or the energy to go watch
superheroes fight CGI monsters the MCU
has been going for so long that it's
become a generational franchise but the
problem is I don't get the impression
the younger generations are all that
interested in it it's why most of their
attempts to pass on iconic superhero
mantles to new characters and actors
have ended in failure because old
audiences won't accept them and new
audiences aren't interested in the first
place i think Marvel knows this too
which is why they're playing the
nostalgia card so hard with this one
time will tell whether it proves to be a
winning hand but personally I don't like
their
odds anyway that's all I've got for
today go away now
